content:
2 min read
Just now

--

I am fascinated by how, in the last couple of weeks, the development network is changing. Suddenly, we have a new role, “Senior AI Engineers”. I always check the network in 2/3 weeks to see where we are going, and right now I skipped more than 4 weeks to check while I was on vacation.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Photo by BoliviaInteligente on UnsplashFunny things that all of the new developers who accepted these new roles have never written a single line in the README.md file in their lives are now experts in MD files.
Here is the truth about how to get a title “Senior AI Engineer”:
1. Use any AI for more than one week.
2. Write 2 or more lines successfully with AI.
You are now officially a “Senior AI Engineer”. Thank me later.
In reality, this is a new technology that we have been using more and more for less than a year. To become a real senior, you need more than 5 years and a lot more experience in new technology, right?
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Photo by Markus Winkler on UnsplashI have a strong argument with this AI word in a title. You are not an AI engineer, you are an LLMs engineer. Get this, did you hear about Hugging Face, Kaggle, Bert model, Tokenisation, Model training, ML?!? How on earth can you be an “AI Engineer”? Then what am I?
Let’s imagine that some engineer from Cursor or Claude who was part of the team that built an AI agent for us wants new opportunities. He/She joins the LinkedIn network and sees a bunch of “Senior AI Engineer”! F**c what we done, what to add as my title?!? Soon he/she will realise that the title after is “Ninja senior AI Engineer”.
How long will it take until we see “Ninja senior AI Engineers”?
